title: Efficient in vivo cytosine base editing using virus-like particles with uracil DNA glycosylase inhibition - Nature Biotechnology
text:
AbstractVirus-like particles (VLPs) are promising for delivering genome editors, yet the in vivo in vivo efficacy of VLP-mediated cytosine base editing remains limited. Here we identified insufficient inhibition of uracil DNA glycosylases as the underlying mechanism of low cytosine base editor (CBE) editing efficiencies in vivo. We engineered a previously reported CBE, transformer base editor (tBE), and developed a VLP delivery system to enhance the recruitment of uracil DNA glycosylase inhibitor proteins. tBE-VLPs achieved robust C-to-T editing in mouse liver and retina. A single injection achieved, on average, 46.0% editing at mPcsk9 and 64.2% at mHpd in the liver, as well as 24.2% at mVegfa in the retinal pigment epithelium, resulting in marked therapeutic benefits in mouse disease models. tBE-VLP4 induced no detectable off-target edits in vitro or in vivo and demonstrated superior specificity compared to AAV or lipid nanoparticle mRNA delivery. Our work establishes tBE-VLP4 as a precise, efficient system for in vivo cytosine base editing.

Data availability

All deep sequencing data and whole-transcriptome sequencing data can be accessed from the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) under BioProjects PRJNA1333683 and PRJNA1334520, respectively. Human and mouse genome sequences were downloaded from University of California, Santa Cruz (UCSC) Genome Browser (https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/bigZips/hg38.fa.gz and https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/mm10/bigZips/mm10.fa.gz). The single-nucleotide variant (SNV) information for the GATK BaseRecalibrator of humans was downloaded from the Single-Nucleotide Polymorphism database through the NCBI (ftp://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/snp/organisms/human_9606_b151_GRCh38p7/VCF/GATK/All_20180418.vcf.gz) and SNV information for GATK BaseRecalibrator of mice was downloaded from the European Variation Archive through the European Molecular Biology Laboratory–European Bioinformatics Institute (EMBL–EBI) (ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/eva/rs_releases/release_1/by_species/Mouse_10090/GRCm38.p4/GCA_000001635.6_current_ids.vcf.gz). Human and mouse repeat regions were downloaded from UCSC Genome Browser (http://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/database/rmsk.txt.gz and http://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/mm10/database/rmsk.txt.gz). GATK VariantRecalibrator for humans, including multiple VCF files, is available online (https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser/_details/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/hapmap_3.3.hg38.vcf.gz, https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser/_details/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/1000G_omni2.5.hg38.vcf.gz, https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser/_details/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/1000G_phase1.snps.high_confidence.hg38.vcf.gz, https://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/snp/organisms/human_9606/VCF/00-All.vcf.gz and https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser/_details/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/Mills_and_1000G_gold_standard.indels.hg38.vcf.gz). GATK VariantRecalibrator for mouse, including VCF files, is also available online (http://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/eva/rs_releases/release_1/by_species/Mouse_10090/GRCm38.p4/GCA_000001635.6_current_ids.vcf.gz). Files used to filter out the background variants for human are available for download (https://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/snp/organisms/human_9606/VCF/00-All.vcf.gz and http://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/database/rmsk.txt.gz). Files used to filter out the background variants for mice are also available for download (http://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/eva/rs_releases/release_1/by_species/Mouse_10090/GRCm38.p4/GCA_000001635.6_current_ids.vcf.gz and http://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/mm10/database/rmsk.txt.gz). Source data are provided with this paper.
Code availability

The custom Perl and Shell scripts for calculating frequencies of base substitution were adapted from previously published code2 and are available from GitHub (https://github.com/YangLab/CFBI).

Additional informationPublisher’s note Springer Nature remains neutral with regard to jurisdictional claims in published maps and institutional affiliations.Extended dataExtended Data Fig. 1 VLP packaging strategy for tBE-V5.a, Schematic of the packaging system of tBE_V5-hVLP. VSV-G, vesicular stomatitis viral glycoprotein; HIVgag, HIV Gag polyprotein including matrix, capsid and nucleocapsid; HIVpol, HIV Pol polyprotein including protease, reverse transcriptase and integrase; NES, nuclear export signal; HIV_PCS, HIV protease cleavage site; NLS, nuclear localization signal; nCas9, Cas9 nickase D10A; 2A, 2A self-cleaving peptides; MCP, bacteriophage MS2 coat protein; UGI, uracil-DNA glycosylase inhibitor; mA3CDA1, mouse APOBEC3 cytidine deaminase domain 1; TEV site, TEV protease cleavage site; mA3dCDI, deoxycytidine deaminase inhibitor from mouse APOBEC3; N22p, the 22 amino acid RNA-binding domain of the λ phage N protein; TEVc, the C-terminal fragment of TEV protease; TEVn, the N-terminal fragment of TEV protease; hsgRNA, helper single guide RNA; MS2, MS2 RNA aptamer; boxB, boxB aptamer. b, Schematic illustrating the design principle of VLP-delivered tBE_V5 at the on-target site. c, Representative western blot analysis of the expression of MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1-TS-mA3dCDI-NLS and MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1 fusion protein in VLPs. P17 was used as the sample processing control. The blots for Anti-MCP and Anti-P17 were performed with the same samples but run in different gels. The theoretical molecular weight of MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1-TS-mA3dCDI-NLS fusion protein is 82.8 kDa. The theoretical molecular weight of MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1 fusion protein is 54.5 kDa. The uncropped blots are provided in Source Data. Representative blots from three independent experiments with similar results are shown. d, Integrated density quantification of the cleaved MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1 fraction in VLPs from western blots. e, Potential mechanism of the MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1-TS-mA3dCDI-NLS fusion protein cleavage in tBE_V5-hVLP. f, Representative western blot analysis of the expression of MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1-TS-mA3dCDI-NLS and MCP-NLS-UGI-mA3CDA1 fusion proteins in tBE_V5-hVLP producer cells.
